$comment: Ch 4 — THE GLASS, scenario 1 (wing bank). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. Engine has no reflection physics; the bank is carried by lesson copy + visual treatment + the GLASS flavor when the answer naturally clips the backboard rect.

meta.title: OFF THE SQUARE.
meta.subtitle: CH 4 — THE GLASS, WING BANK
meta.authorIntent: The introductory bank from the 45° wing. d=5.0, h_r=2.2, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=58°, v=7.81 m/s. Verification: t = 5.0 / (7.81·cos 58°) = 1.208 s; y(1.208) = 2.2 + 7.81·sin(58°)·1.208 − ½·9.8·1.208² = 3.050 m — matches h_h (err 0.000 m). Pedagogical purpose: prove the square is an aiming guide, not decoration. Direct shots from this angle are narrow; the board widens the target. Higher-arc answer (58°) so the ball can plausibly kiss the glass on its way down — engine scores it GLASS if it does.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short of the glass entirely.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The aim missed the square low.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Aim higher on the board.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Hit the board too hard.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long off the board. Too much pace.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. Nothing on the square, nothing on the iron.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the square.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=58°, v=7.81 m/s. High enough arc to plausibly kiss the glass on the way down — when it does, the engine flavors it GLASS naturally.

hints.body: The ball still moves on a parabola. The backboard just gives it a second target.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Aim a touch high so the ball can kiss the glass.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 58° — a higher arc lets the ball drop onto the square. Solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.81 m/s.

intro.headline: OFF THE SQUARE.
intro.subhead: From the wing. The board is the target, not the rim.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Cleared the rim without touching glass. Rare.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Aimed at the square. Square delivered.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Glass, then iron, then in. Banked the long way.

miss.headline: WIDE OF THE BOARD

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. The aim missed the square low.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Hit the board too hard.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short of the glass entirely.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long off the board. Too much pace.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. Nothing on the square, nothing on the iron.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 58°, push v near 7.8.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 58°, cut v by ~0.8.
bracketHintByCategory.AIRBALL: Geometry: θ near 58°, v near 7.8 toward the square.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
